title: podman ps --filter changed behavior between  podman 3:3.1.2-3.fc34 and 3:3.2.0-5.fc34
body:
/kind bug


**Description**
Filter became now more sensitive and wants to use also a registry and tag:

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Now (3.2.0-5):

``` bash
[root@localhost ~]# podman ps --quiet --all --filter ancestor=cockpit/ws

[root@localhost ~]# podman ps --quiet --all --filter ancestor=quay.io/cockpit/ws:latest
91bdb43aa605

```

Previously:
``` bash
[root@localhost ~]# podman ps --quiet --all --filter ancestor=cockpit/ws
ef278d107275

[root@localhost ~]# podman ps --quiet --all --filter ancestor=quay.io/cockpit/ws:latest
ef278d107275
```

comments.body: @vrothberg This looks like an image library thing?

I quickly bisected it: 
```
b0ea40f551aa902f3bf0b590af24f911130328e9 is the first bad commit
commit b0ea40f551aa902f3bf0b590af24f911130328e9
Author: flouthoc <flouthoc.git@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 18 17:56:10 2021 +0530

    Fix podman ps --filter ancestor to match exact ImageName/ImageID
    
    Signed-off-by: flouthoc <flouthoc.git@gmail.com>

```
Ahhhh - I remember now. I believe this was deliberate (the filter should not have been that liberal in matching its arguments).
Alright, docker had this 'new' behavior as well. If it's expected and you think it's the correct behavior feel free to close.
